summary: All 4 attribution methods (EK-FAC IF, K-FAC IF, RepSim, TRAK) successfully computed valid per-point rankings for 100 test points on 5K train subset. However, J@10(EK-FAC, K-FAC) std=0.031 (below 0.05 threshold) because using only layer4+fc makes the K-FAC/EK-FAC eigenvalue correction negligible. Full-model computation is required to see meaningful divergence.

pass_criteria_evaluation.jaccard_std_above_005: FAIL (0.031 < 0.05, but expected with layer4+fc only)

critical_findings: K-FAC and EK-FAC rankings are nearly identical (J@10=0.995) at layer4+fc scope because B-matrix eigenvalues are ~0, making eigenvalue correction negligible
Full-model Hessian is essential: the K-FAC/EK-FAC gap manifests primarily in earlier layers where Kronecker factorization approximation diverges more
EK-FAC and RepSim are highly dissimilar (J@10=0.017, tau=-0.09), confirming these methods capture fundamentally different attribution signals
Both IF methods agree strongly with TRAK (LDS=0.74), validating the attribution pipeline
GPU memory constraint (~7GB occupied by another user's process) prevents dattri vmap-based full-model computation on RTX 4090
requirements_for_full_experiment: Dedicated RTX 4090 (24GB) with no memory sharing, OR A6000 (48GB available on GPU 0)
dattri IFAttributorEKFAC was verified feasible at 10.43GB peak during phase1_setup (with full GPU)
TRAK-50 requires retraining ResNet-18 with 50 checkpoint saves
Full 50K training set attribution computation (~5 GPU-hours estimated)
